item.title: China’s State Grid Pledges $4.5 Billion for Pumped Hydro in 2026
item.description: China’s largest grid operator has pledged to spend 31 billion yuan ($4.5 billion) on pumped hydro storage this year, in support of the company’s plan to lift total capacity by more than 70% by the end of the decade.

item.title: Rogoff Calls Dollar 20% Overvalued, Warns Markets ‘Naive’ on War
item.description: Elevated US dollar valuations signal long-term correction risks, according to Harvard University professor Kenneth Rogoff, who also warned that investors may be overestimating the likelihood of a near-term resolution to the Iran war.

item.title: Hungary Will Reclaim Oil, Pharma Shares From Pro-Orban Academy
item.description: Hungary’s incoming leader plans to renationalize shares handed by outgoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban to an academic foundation set up to propagate his “illiberal democracy” ideology.

item.title: War in Iran Is Already Reshaping East Asia's Energy Future
item.description: A Conversation with Alex Turnbull

item.title: War in Iran Is Already Reshaping East Asia’s Energy Future
item.description: The war in Iran has caused the price of all kinds of commodities to surge, and that has a negative economic impact almost everywhere. But the squeeze is really being felt hard in East Asia, which is the ultimate destination for a lot of oil and gas that come out of the Gulf. And though the Strait of Hormuz may eventually re-open, and the acute pain may pass, this episode may already be reshaping the future. On this episode of the podcast we speak with Alex Turnbull, an investor based in Singapor

item.title: Greece Is Set to Reveal Yet More Outperformance on Budget Goals
item.description: Greece probably outperformed key fiscal metrics for 2025 by at least one percentage point of output, offering leeway for aid to voters facing a souring economy, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Norway’s Crude Exports Hit Record Value as Oil Price Soared
item.description: Norway’s crude exports surged to a record by value last month due to the outbreak of the war in the Middle East, helping to raise the country’s trade surplus to the highest level in more than three years.

item.title: GE, India’s HAL Sign Tech Deal to Advance Joint Jet Engine Plan
item.description: Indian and US companies reached a technology agreement to jointly manufacture jet engines, a first for both, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi seeks foreign partners to modernize the air force and produce weapons in the South Asian nation.

item.title: Chinese Nickel Tycoon Taps Top Global Traders for Aluminum Push
item.description: A Chinese tycoon who helped transform Indonesia’s metals industry over the past decade is in talks with commodity trading giants Mercuria Energy Group, Glencore Plc and Trafigura Group to secure investments in his new, $3 billion aluminum smelter.

item.title: Russia Shuts Down $13 Billion Tax Fraud, Investigators Say
item.description: Russian investigators said they broke up an organized crime group in Moscow that was responsible for more than 1 trillion rubles ($13.3 billion) in tax losses to the state budget, which is already strained by a widening deficit.

item.title: Trump Says End to Iran War in Sight, Spurring Market Rally
item.description: US President Donald Trump indicated he may be preparing to wind down the war with Iran, boosting market optimism and restoring some stability to global energy prices.

item.title: Bessent and UK’s Reeves at Odds Over Iran War Ahead of Meeting
item.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told British media that the Iran war is worth a “small bit of economic pain,” ahead of what is likely to be a tense meeting with his UK counterpart Rachel Reeves.

item.title: French Inflation Was Faster Than Initially Thought in March
item.description: France’s inflation rate was higher than first thought last month as energy costs surged due to the war in Iran.

item.title: Indian Small-Cap Stocks Outpace Larger Peers to Erase War Losses
item.description: Small-cap stocks have stood out in India, erasing losses from the US-Iran war even as larger peers lag.

item.title: UK Recruiters Warn of Unclear Outlook as War Hurts Hiring Market
item.description: UK recruiters said the conflict in the Middle East may hurt global recruitment markets, dimming the outlook just as hiring started to pick up in some geographies.

item.title: Indonesia’s Rating Most at Risk in Southeast Asia Amid War, S&P Says
item.description: Sovereign ratings in Southeast Asia are expected to come under strain, with Indonesia’s particularly at risk in case of a prolonged Middle East conflict, S&P Global Ratings said.

item.title: European Stock Rally Halts as Earnings Kick Into Gear
Italy Gives €211 Million to Data Center Tech Firm 2D Photonics
item.description: Italy awarded a €211 million ($249 million) grant to a company seeking to speed up processing at artificial intelligence data centers as part of a bid to support local startups.

item.title: Victory Giant Is Said Set to Price Hong Kong Listing at Top of Range
item.description: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. is likely to price its Hong Kong listing at the highest possible figure, according to people familiar with the matter, in a sign of strong demand for the city’s biggest share sale in seven months.

item.title: ASML Raises 2026 Sales Forecast as AI Demand Fuels Growth
item.description: ASML Holding NV raised its full-year sales forecast as the surge in global artificial intelligence spending fuels semiconductor production and boosts demand for the company’s advanced chipmaking machines.

item.title: Japan May Extend $10 Billion to Southeast Asia to Help Buy Oil
item.description: Japan plans to provide as much as $10 billion in financial support to nations in Southeast Asia to help them cope with soaring crude oil prices due to the war in the Middle East, according to local media reports.

item.title: South Korea’s Naver Prices First Euro Bond Amid Push Into AI
item.description: South Korean platform giant Naver Corp. has sold a combined $1.1 billion of green international bonds, including its inaugural euro-denominated bond, as the company continues its bet on artificial-intelligence investments.

item.title: Singapore Home Sales Rebound as Project Launches Attract Buyers
item.description: Singapore new home sales rose in March as fresh project launches drew buyers back to the market after a slow start to the year.

item.title: India’s RBI Holds Talks With Banks on Ways to Boost Deposits
item.description: India’s central bank is seeking input from commercial lenders on how they can bring in larger and more stable deposits, as a shift in household savings into equities and other investment products threatens to snowball into a bigger problem for the country’s banks.

item.title: Yellen Still Sees Chance of a Fed Cut This Year Amid Oil Shock
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary and ex-Federal Reserve Chair Janet Yellen said she still sees prospects for a US interest-rate cut later this year, though the unfolding oil shock caused by the war in Iran clouds the outlook.

item.title: Asset Management One CEO Says Non-Japan Funds to Double by 2028
item.description: Mizuho Financial Group Inc.’s money management arm expects strong demand from overseas investors looking to Japan to drive asset growth in the coming years.

item.title: Lululemon Sparks China Social Media Debate On ‘Forever Chemicals’
item.description: A Texas investigation into Lululemon Athletica Inc.’s clothing for the presence of so-called “forever chemicals” has sparked a wave of debate across Chinese social media, underscoring the risks the brand faces in one of its most important growth markets.

item.title: For Markets, the War Might as Well Be Over
item.description: Stocks are nearly back to their all-time highs from the months before the conflict.

item.title: Nvidia’s New AI Models Spark Rally in Quantum Computing Stocks
item.description: Asian software and information-technology stocks surged after Nvidia Corp. unveiled a suite of new open-source AI models aimed at accelerating progress within quantum computing.

item.title: Japan’s Asahi Kasei Says Seeking Alternative Sources of Naphtha
item.description: Japanese chemicals maker Asahi Kasei Corp. says it’s working to diversify sources of naphtha as the war in the Middle East curbs supplies.
